title: Markdown Block
content:
Which Markdown specification is used in Pushword — CommonMark, GFM, or something else?

The default Markdown implementation in Pushword is based on CommonMark, with a few customizations designed to make it easy to switch between

title: Upgrade Guide
content:
Smooth way is to use composer, a dependency manager for PHP.

Run composer update and the job is done (almost).

If you are doing a major upgrade, find the upgrade guide down there.

To 1.0.0-rc80

content:

title: Static Generator
content:
Generate a static website serve by github pages, apaches with one command or via the admin.

Install

composer require pushword/static-generator

That's it !

title: Installation
content:
Requirements

  • PHP >=8.4
  • PHP extensions : dom, curl, libxml, mbstring, zip, pdo, sqlite, pdo_sqlite, bcmath, soap, intl, gd (or imagick), exif, iconv, fileinfo
  • Composer -

title: Version
content:
Versioning pages with Pushword CMS.

Install

composer require pushword/version

Add Routes

admin:
  resource: '@PushwordVersionBundle/VersionRoutes.yaml'
# or do it in 1 command

title: Command
content:
Let's take a look at the commands available in Pushword and their purpose. Keep in mind that the exact list may vary depending on the installed extensions.

 pushword
  pw:flat:sync                Import to database or Export to flat

title: Entity Filter
content:
The Entity Filter may be the most powerful thing built for Pushword.

It's offer to apply what you want on properties from a class (mostly entity and often mostly Page).

content:
Supercharge the Pushword Admin with a new admin form field to customize the main image format from not visible to HERO + default template files.
